page: OVHcloud t2-le-45 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server instance configured on the KVM hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6226R processor with 15 physical cores operating at 2.9 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration provides a single thread per core with no hyperthreading. Memory resources total 45.0 GB, yielding a ratio of 3.0 GB per core. For acceleration, the instance includes a single NVIDIA Volta V100S GPU with 32 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 300 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is rated at a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate average CPU capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1210.0 and a multi-core score of 8924.0. The Passmark CPU score is 22172.73. However, the integrated NVIDIA V100S GPU delivers strong results in specialized workloads. In LLM inference tests, the instance achieves top-tier performance for prompt processing with a 7B model at 1434.01 tokens per second, and text generation at 124.44 tokens per second. For smaller 135M models, prompt processing reaches 19245.13 tokens per second. Performance drops significantly on larger 70B models, where prompt processing is limited to 14.88 tokens per second, reflecting the memory constraints of the 32 GB VRAM.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the t2-le-45 offers a balanced entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads without the premium associated with newer-generation architectures. The inclusion of dedicated physical cores and local NVMe storage reduces virtualization overhead and I/O bottlenecks. Tradeoffs include the older Volta GPU architecture and moderate single-core CPU performance. This server is configured for workloads such as medium-scale LLM inference, machine learning model training, data science tasks, and GPU-accelerated rendering.
description: OVHcloud t2-le-45 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 6226R processor with 15 physical cores at 2.9 GHz and 45.0 GB of RAM. It is equipped with a single NVIDIA Volta V100S GPU offering 32 GB of VRAM, alongside 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show moderate CPU performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8924.0, but strong GPU-driven capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier LLM inference speeds for medium-sized 7B models, reaching 1434.01 tokens per second for prompt processing. While the older Volta architecture presents a generational tradeoff, the dedicated hardware profile provides a cost-efficient platform for machine learning inference, data science, and parallel computing workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud t2-le-45 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 15 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 45 GB RAM, a 32 GB NVIDIA V100S GPU, and 300 GB NVMe storage, optimized for medium-scale machine learning workloads.

tagline: A dedicated GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA V100S GPU and NVMe storage for machine learning and parallel computing workloads.

NVIDIA Volta V100S GPU with 32 GB VRAM for accelerated computing.
